name: Retool
what_it_does: Low-code platform for building internal tools and admin panels by connecting to databases and APIs, with a REST API for managing apps, workflows, and user access programmatically.
use_cases: Programmatically creating and deploying Retool apps for multi-tenant internal tool workflows
Managing user access and team permissions for Retool deployments via API
Triggering Retool workflows from external systems as part of automation pipelines
Querying audit logs for compliance monitoring of internal tool usage
Automating resource group and environment management in enterprise Retool deployments
not_for: Customer-facing applications (Retool is for internal tools; use a proper frontend framework)
High-traffic public applications (Retool has per-user pricing that becomes expensive at scale)
Teams without a software engineering culture — low-code still requires programming knowledge
Real-time applications requiring sub-100ms response times
best_when: Your engineering team needs to build admin panels and internal CRUD tools quickly without a full frontend development investment.
avoid_when: You're building customer-facing products, need highly customized UI, or have a large number of users that makes per-seat pricing prohibitive.

auth.notes: API keys created in Retool settings. Account-level access — no resource-level scoping. Store securely as keys provide full admin access to the Retool organization.

pricing.notes: Per-seat pricing can become expensive for large internal teams. Self-hosted (Retool On-Premise) available for enterprise customers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP codes. Error messages are functional but the Management API is less documented than Retool's core product.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: App creation is not idempotent. Most update operations by resource ID are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: The Retool Management API is separate from Retool Workflows — don't confuse the two APIs
App resources use UUID identifiers — store after creation for future reference
Self-hosted and Cloud instances may have different API versions and capabilities
Retool Workflows (automation product) has its own separate trigger API
Permission changes may require page refresh in Retool UI to take effect — propagation can lag
